title: Hut 8 Closes $3.25 Billion of Investment-Grade Senior Secured Notes in Landmark Financing for River Bend Data Center Project
text: Hut 8 closed a $3.25 billion offering of 6.192% senior secured notes due 2042 issued by its wholly-owned subsidiary, Hut 8 DC LLC. The deal is described as the first investment-grade construction bond issued for a single-sponsor data center project. Project Details: The proceeds will finance the development and construction of a turnkey data center with 245 megawatts of critical IT capacity and the related substation at Hut 8's River Bend campus. River Bend is an AI data center campus in West Feliciana Parish, Louisiana, with potential to scale past 1GW of utility capacity. Financing Structure: The Notes are rated BBB− by S&P Global Ratings and BBB− by Fitch Ratings. Hut 8's investment-grade market access supported a 16.5-year tenor matched to the construction period and the 15-year contracted lease term. The offering delivers investment-grade access, no recourse to Hut 8, and no dilution to shareholders. The Issuer intends to make a distribution to Hut 8 of approximately $184 million representing a return of prior equity contributions. Key Partners: The project has involvement from both Google and Anthropic through an agreement backed by Google to lease the campus to AI cloud firm Fluidstack.
